scope: OSV scan findings from package-lock.json for the public CI/deployment toolchain
policy: OSV findings are accepted only when they are transitive development/tooling dependencies that are not linked into Solidity bytecode, are not deployed on-chain, and are not reachable from a public server runtime in this repository. New OSV IDs not listed here remain Tier 1 blockers.
rationale: The listed packages are npm toolchain/transitive dependencies used for local development, Hardhat compilation/tests, audit tooling, or local deployment scripts. They are not Solidity source, not embedded in generated bytecode, not served by a repository web service, and not deployed to Ethereum Mainnet. Mainnet authorization remains blocked if OSV reports any unlisted vulnerability ID or if the scanner fails to execute.
requiredFollowUp: Continue using npm ci from package-lock.json for reproducibility.
Do not run npm audit fix --force without compile/test/security review.
Re-run npm run audit:osv after dependency changes and update this triage only with explicit rationale.
